displayName.description: The display name of the user group.

schemas.description:
Collection of schema URIs that define the structure and valid attributes for this resource.
Each URI represents a schema that determines which properties can be included and how they should be formatted.

active.description: Indicates the current status of the user account. `true` indicates that the user is active.

displayName.description: The display name of a user.

schemas.description: Collection of schema URIs that define the structure and valid attributes for this resource. Each URI represents a schema that determines which properties can be included and how they should be formatted.

userName.description: Must be the same as the primary email address and must be a valid email address. This serves as the unique identifier for the user.

primary.description: A boolean declaring if the email is the primary email or not. The first email provided is considered the primary email.

value.description: The email address of the user. Must be a valid email address.

detail.description: Additional detailed information about the error.
detail.example: Request is unparsable, syntactically incorrect, or violates schema.

scimType.description:
A machine-readable error type that indicates the nature of the error.
This is a SCIM-specific error type.

GLPErrorExtension.description:
The HPE GreenLake error extension defines additional attributes and structure
for error responses in the SCIM API.

debugId.description: A unique identifier for the instance of this error. This can be used to help with troubleshooting.

errorCode.description: A unique machine-friendly, but human-readable identifier for the error.

GroupExtension.description:
The SCIM group extension defines additional attributes and structure
for group resources in the SCIM API. This extension is compliant
with [SCIM 2.0](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7644).

groupDescription.description: A description of the user group. This might include the group's purpose or responsibilities. This helps administrators understand the group's role in the Organization.

hpe_principal.description: The security principal for role assignments.

source.description: The source of the user group, defines where the user's group lifecycle is being controlled from.

sourceInstance.description:
The source instance is set according to the source of the user group.
It will be the following according to the source type:

- **Local**—A group managed by the current organization. 
  - **Source instance**—The current organization's `orgId`.

- **SCIM**—A group managed by a SCIM integration, for example, Azure.
  - **Source instance**—The SCIM Integration's `scimId`.

- **External**—A group managed by a different organization.
  - **Source instance**—The external controlling organization's ID.

Resources.description: A list of user group objects.

itemsPerPage.description: The number of resources returned in a list response page.

schemas.description:
Collection of schema URIs that define the structure and valid attributes for this resource.
Each URI represents a schema that determines which properties can be included and how they should be formatted.

startIndex.description: Specifies the pagination start index. Index starts at 1.

totalResults.description: The total number of results returned by the query including pages not shown.

created.description: The date and time the resource was created.

lastModified.description: The date and time the resource was last modified.

Name.description: An object that includes the given name and family name of a user.

op.description:
Operation performed by patch.
- `add` or `Add`—If the user was already a member of this group, no changes will be made to the resource and a success response will be returned.
- `remove` or `Remove`—If any of the requested users to be removed are not a member of this group, a success (204) response will be returned.
- `replace` or `Replace`—Change the name or description of the group.

path.description: Attribute path describing the target of the operation.

anyOf.description: New value for the attribute being modified.
A list of users to patch the user group with.

items.description: Users to be added to the group or removed from the group.

display.description: The display name of the resource.

schemas.description:
Collection of schema URIs that define the structure and valid attributes for this resource.
Each URI represents a schema that determines which properties can be included and how they should be formatted.

PosixUserExtension.description:
The POSIX user extension enables integration with Unix/Linux systems by providing 
system-level user account attributes. POSIX (Portable Operating System Interface) 
is a standard for Unix-like operating systems. 

If your organization does not use Unix/Linux systems, this extension can be omitted. Posix attributes are disabled by default, but can be included if they're enabled within the target organization.

gid.description:
Primary group identifier for the user on Unix/Linux systems. This numeric 
ID determines the user's default group membership and is used for file 
permissions and access control.

homeDirectory.description:
Full path to the user's home directory on Unix/Linux systems. This is 
where the user's personal files are stored and where they are placed 
when logging in via SSH or terminal.

shell.description:
Default command shell for the user on Unix/Linux systems. This determines 
the command interpreter used when the user logs in via SSH or terminal. 
Common shells include ``/bin/bash``, ``/bin/sh``, or ``/bin/zsh``.

uid.description:
Numeric user identifier used by Unix/Linux systems to identify users at the 
operating system level. This number (typically 1000+) is used for file 
ownership and process permissions. Required for users who need access to 
Unix/Linux systems or file shares.

userName.description:
Login name for the user on Unix/Linux systems. This is the name used for 
SSH connections, terminal sessions, and system authentication. Should be 
lowercase and contain only alphanumeric characters and underscores.

displayName.description:
Unique display name for the user group. A valid `displayName` can contain alphanumeric characters, space, 
      or any of the following: `-`, `/`, `_`, `.`, `!`, `(`, `)`, `[`, `]`, `<`, `>`

id.description: The unique identifier for an HPE GreenLake user group.

schemas.description:
Collection of schema URIs that define the structure and valid attributes for this resource.
Each URI represents a schema that determines which properties can be included and how they should be formatted.

SCIMUser.description:
The SCIM user schema defines the attributes and structure for user resources
in the SCIM API. This schema is compliant with [SCIM 2.0](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7644).

active.description: A boolean indicating whether the user is active in the system. When `true`, the user is active.

displayName.description: The display name for the user.

id.description: HPE GreenLake global user ID unique resource identifier randomly generated by the system.

schemas.description: Collection of URIs that identify the schemas used to define the structure and attributes of this User resource. These schemas determine which properties are valid for the resource.

userName.description: Must be the same as work type email address.

UserCreateExtension.description:
The SCIM user creation extension defines additional attributes and structure
for creating user resources in the SCIM API. This extension is compliant
with [SCIM 2.0](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7644).

countryCode.description: Specifies the user's country using a two-letter ISO country code.

hpe_principal.description: The unique identifier of the security principal for role-based access control (RBAC) assignments.

primaryEmailVerified.description: A boolean declaring if the user's primary email was verified. `true` if the email is verified.

status.description: Indicates the current status of the user account. This field is automatically managed by the system based on the user's lifecycle events.

UserExtension.description: The SCIM user extension defines additional attributes and structure for user resources in the SCIM API. This extension is compliant with [SCIM 2.0](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7644).

countryCode.description: Specifies the user's country using a two-letter ISO code.

hpe_principal.description: The security principal for role assignments.

primaryEmailVerified.description: A boolean declaring if the user's primary email was verified. `true` if the email is verified.

source.description: The source of the user, defines where the user's lifecycle is being controlled from.

sourceInstance.description:
The source instance is set according to the source of the user.
It will be the following according to the source type:

- **Local**—A user managed by the current organization. 
  A user was directly added to the organization from the **Organization users** page on the platform. 
  Alternatively, A user was automatically added to the organization
    when they were added to an organization workspace.
  - **Source instance**—The current organization's `orgId`.

- **SCIM**—Users were added to the organization through a SCIM integration with an external IdP. For example, Azure.
  - **Source instance**—The SCIM integration's `scimId`

- **External**—A user managed by a different organization. Users were automatically added to the organization when they were added to an organization workspace through propagation from an external organization.
  - **Source instance**—The external controlling organization's `orgId`.

status.description: Indicates the current status of the user account.

created.description: The date and time the resource was created.

lastLogin.description: The date and time the user last logged in.

lastModified.description: The date and time the resource was last modified.

Operations.description: List of operations to be performed on the user. See [RFC 7644, Section 3.5.2](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7644#section-3.5.2).

schemas.description: Collection of schema URIs that define the structure and valid attributes for this resource. Each URI represents a schema that determines which properties can be included and how they should be formatted.

op.description:
Operations performed by patch.
  - `add` or `Add`—The user object has the specified attribute added to it or updated if it already exists.
  - `remove` or `Remove`—The user object has the specified attribute removed from it.
  - `replace` or `Replace`—The user object has the specified attribute replaced with the new value.

path.description:
Attribute path describing the target of the operation. 
This optional attribute must be only one path to a valid target in 
the user schemas.

value.description: The value of the attribute or attributes to be added, removed, or replaced.

itemsPerPage.description: The number of resources returned in a list response page.

schemas.description: Collection of schema URIs that define the structure and valid attributes for this resource.

startIndex.description: Specifies the pagination start index. Index starts at 1.

totalResults.description: The total number of results returned by the query including pages not shown.

Bearer.description: Access token compliant with [RFC8725](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8725). Issued by HPE GreenLake.

info.description: User and Group Management APIs compliant with [SCIM 2.0](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7644).
info.title: HPE GreenLake SCIM User and Group Management APIs

get.description: Get a list of user groups. Compliant with [SCIM 2.0](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7644).

parameters.description:
Return only the subset of groups that match the filter. The filter grammar is a subset of OData 4.0.

**NOTE:** The filter query parameter must use [URL encoding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/URL_encoding). Most clients do this automatically with inputs provided to them specifically as query parameters. Encoding must be done manually for any query parameters provided as part of the URL. The reserved characters `!` `#` `$` `&` `'` `(` `)` `*` `+` `,` `/` `:` `;` `=` `?` `@` `[` `]` must be encoded with percent encoded equivalents.

**The Get Groups API filters enabled are**: Supported filters: - `displayName` (operators: `sw`, `eq`, `co`) - `urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extensions:hpe-greenlake:2.0:Group:source` (operators: `eq`)

Equals Filter with displayName.description: Search for groups whose display name exactly matches the specified text. This filter is useful for finding specific groups when you know the exact group name, ensuring exact matches and no partial results.
Equals Filter with displayName.summary: Use the equals filter with displayName

Equals Filter with source.summary: Use the equals filter with source

StartsWith Filter with displayName.description: Search for groups whose display names start with specific text. Useful for finding groups by specific prefixes or searching for groups with common naming patterns.
StartsWith Filter with displayName.summary: Use the StartsWith filter with displayName

parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Number of results to return in a page.
parameters.name: count

parameters.description: Specifies the pagination start index. Index starts at 1.
parameters.name: startIndex

200.description: Returned a list of user groups.

409.description: Conflict. Group with given name already exists.

delete.description: Delete a user group. Compliant with [SCIM 2.0](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7644).

patch.description: Add or remove users from a user group, or update the group name or description. Compliant with [SCIM 2.0](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7644).

parameters.description: The HPE GreenLake user group ID. Retrieve the ID from the `CREATE Group` endpoint or the `GET Group` endpoint.
parameters.name: groupId

requestBody.description: Add or remove users from a user group, or update the group name or description. The replace operation is not supported for group members.

parameters.description:
Return only the subset of users that match the filter. The filter grammar is a subset of OData 4.0.

**NOTE:** The filter query parameter must use [URL encoding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/URL_encoding). Most clients do this automatically with inputs provided to them specifically as query parameters. Encoding must be done manually for any query parameters provided as part of the URL. The reserved characters `!` `#` `$` `&` `'` `(` `)` `*` `+` `,` `/` `:` `;` `=` `?` `@` `[` `]` must be encoded with percent encoded equivalents.

**The Get Users API filters enabled are**: - displayName - userName **Supported operators**: - sw (starts with) - eq (equals) - co (contains)

Filter contains displayName.description: Find users with specific text in their user name. For example, searching for "admin" is useful for identifying administrative accounts across the organization.

Filter contains userName.description: Find users with specific text anywhere in their user name (email address). This search is useful for identifying users with common email domains or patterns in their email addresses, such as finding all users with "admin" or team identifiers in their addresses.

Filter equals displayName.description: Search for users whose display name exactly matches the specified text. This filter is useful for finding specific users when you know the exact display name, ensuring exact matches and no partial results.

Filter equals userName.description: Search for users whose user name (email address) exactly matches the specified text. This filter is useful for finding a specific user when you know their exact email address, ensuring precise user identification.

Filter starts with displayName.description: Search for users whose display names start with specific text. Useful for finding users by specific prefixes or searching for users with common name patterns.

Filter starts with userName.description: Search for users whose user names start with specific text. Useful for finding users by specific prefixes or searching for users with common name patterns.

Filter with both displayName and userNam.description: Returns users whose email or name start with "sample" text.
Filter with both displayName and userNam.summary: Filter with both displayName and userName
